old_claim.note: Lasers is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: DWDM channels are optical carriers at specific wavelengths; practical systems use controlled laser transmitters for those channels.

source_shape.source_locator: Cisco Introduction to DWDM Technology describes wavelength-specific optical channels and DWDM transmitters/receivers in fiber transport systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Practical DWDM systems require wavelength-controlled optical transmitters, modeled here through the lasers prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports keeping the edge required while replacing the generic inferred note with a concrete optical-carrier requirement.

ontology_before: The graph had the right broad edge type but only inferred support.
ontology_after: The graph keeps lasers as required and gives the edge a source-backed wavelength-channel rationale.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: dense_wavelength_division_multiplexing -> lasers remains required; changed: evidence is source-backed.
would_reject_if: The node were scoped to passive wavelength filters alone rather than full DWDM transport systems.
A more specific tunable-laser or optical-transmitter node replaced the broad lasers prerequisite.
short_reason: DWDM optical carriers require controlled laser transmitters.
